17
The choice of your Q4 strategy depends on:
● Nature of your business
● Objectives (growth and scale OR predictable stability)
● Your budget
● Marketing assets
● Targeting assets
● Teams bandwidth to make creatives
● When you start preparing (your runway).
Choosing your Q4 strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 53 --

18
Let’s acknowledge that different business need different strategies in Q4:
1. For many - especially in eCommerce, it's the make or break period of the whole year. 💸
2. For others, they experience a slow down in sales activity- common in many B2B companies.⏳
3. Non-christmassy product or service (e.g. subscription boxes, info products).
Choosing your Q4 strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 53 --

19
If you’re advertising for a B2B company:
● Seriously consider running a minimal budget in Q4.
● If you have a yearly ad budget, then rather spend more of it now in Q3.
● Pull back on prospecting in Q4, especially in phase 2 & 3 of Q4.
● Instead of Q4 focus on January in Q1. That's a great time for audience creation in B2B.
Choosing your Q4 strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 53 --

20
If you’re advertising for a non-christmassy product or service:
● Go for creative solutions or exclusive offers. E.g. Christmas edition box of beer as a gift.
● The bulletproof bet - gift cards.
● Choose to go against the masses.
Choosing your Q4 strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 53 --

21
Choose to go against the masses example:
● Open Wear - sustainable outerwear company.
Choosing your Q4 strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 53 --

22
If you’re advertising for a eCommerce company:
● Here is where we set the focus.
● Let’s walk through each phase of Q4 from the eCommerce lens.
Choosing your Q4 strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 53 --

23
Phase 2: Build momentum
Period: September to mid-November
● Competition is lower = it's easier and less costly to get people into your funnel.
● Your goal in this phase is to get discovered - get new people to your site.
● Think of it as audience building. Meaning you want to bring quality buyers to your site (less focus on ROAS).
To elaborate on: "Anything not tested ahead of October, forget it."
● The point is that you have your audiences and creative dialled in before the end of October.
● Meaning you have a working funnel and you've completed your audience building.
● Phase 2 is the last time to feed the funnel and build audiences.
Your focus per/phasewww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 53 --

24
Phase 3: Maximize sales
Period: Mid-November to the last pre-Christmas shipping date (For most brand, this is December 20th)
● Online consumer activity is peaking during phase 3. Consumers are expecting an offer and they are keen to shop &
purchase.
● Avoid prospecting and focus your effort on retargeting of the audiences generated in phase 2.
● It's too expensive to acquire new audience and you certainly don't want to be running test campaigns.
This means:
● Generating repeated purchases - cross-selling & upselling. Getting folks who bought in phase 1 and 2 to buy again.
● Retargeting MOFU and BOFU audiences.
● At that point you need to work with what you got.
Your focus per/phasewww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 53 --

25
Heads-up regarding the start of phase 3
Don’t panic, if you experience a drop in sales roundabout mid-November 😱😱😱
● Expect a delayed attribution from Nov onwards.
● People know that the biggest sale of the year is coming up, so they 'window shop' a lot more and plan to come back the
day of the sales.
● Consumers have been trained, they know when they'll receive the best offers.
Your focus per/phasewww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 53 --

26
Phase 4: Post-holiday sales
Period: Whole January (optionally also February)
● It's a period were the Christmas present are bought - people have sorted out their budgets and there is some money left
over.
● It's a moment in time where the CPM on Facebook is the lowest it's going to be in the whole year.
Don't forget that:
● Tons of people have gotten gift cards as present and they're keen to sort them out & buy.
● Gifting plain money is ever more popular - folks have cash at hand.
● In the US people start receiving tax returns form February.
● People locked down their spendable income in gifts and holiday travels in Nov/Dec - now they have new spendable income
at hand.
● In Northern Hemisphere it's a really cold & dark time. Thus, convince shopping - eCommerce is what people love.
Your focus per/phasewww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

30
Conclusion:
● Facebook is not the be all and end all for growth, know how to diversify.
● It's not Newton physic, it's not a linear buying journey.
● People bounce around to multiple channels and those frequently include social and search.
● Expand your traffic sources.
● Visibility on Amazon is now a must (and don’t forget Google).
Overarching Q4 Strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 53 --

31
Ensure that your Google and Facebook strategy works as ONE united flank.
Overarching Q4 Strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 53 --

32
Usa a seasonal source for a LAL
● For holiday campaigns (E.g. Mothers day, Valentines Day, Christmas etc.) you want to use seasonal data, as a source for
your LAL.
● P.S. Assuming you have enough data to go to granular.
Why?
● This way you capture people who qualify themselves us Holiday shopper.
● It's the "people like us, do things like this" principle.
Example:
● For a Christmas campaign source you could pull data from Thanksgiving to Boxing Day. 22nd of Nov - 24th Dec.
Q4 Targetings Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 53 --

33
Tip for Phase 2 targeting - VIP offer list
● Works like a charm for audience building.
● Gather audiences by creating a VIP offer list.
● The point is to generate exclusiveness: "Deals are coming. Be a part of our VIP list to get access first!"
● Backtracking on the trend mentioned earlier - consumers know when they'll receive the best offers.
● To create a VIP list, set up a dedicated landing page.
Q4 Targetings Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 53 --

34
Tip for Phase 3 targeting - narrow the time intervals for CA-s
● If your objective is sales at scale, not predictability (e.g. eCommerce), then tighten your time windows for custom
audiences.
● You're going to receive lot more traffic in phase 3.
● Plus those folks are keen to buy, they know very well that it’s the last moment + best prices.
Q4 Targetings Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 53 --

35
Let’s be real.
● These days with the Facebook Algorithm getting so good with targeting. The secret serious scaling is that audiences are not
your biggest barrier to growth, creatives and offer angles are.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

36
Let’s be real V2

Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

38
Tip1: Reframe your product as a natural gift.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 53 --

39
Tip 1: Reframe your product as a natural gift.
● Tinuiti - "The consistent top-3 gifting categories across age groups include clothing, electronics, and gift cards."
● Backtracking to "pull marketing".
● If you struggle to reframe your product, then gift cards should be the front and centre. They are especially good for
last-minute gift buyers - offering them salvation.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 53 --

40
Tip 2: Polish up your return policy.
● Holiday returns are the new normal.
● Tinuiti - "We found that 40% of shoppers returned at least one gift they bought or received in 2018".
● A hassle-free, clearly-stated return policy is a purchase differentiator.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 53 --

41
Tip 3: Make free wrapping the star of your campaigns.
● Free wrapping = job done in people minds.
● Convenience - that's what people want.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 41 of 53 --

42
Tip 4: Leverage gift guides.
● If done well, they are very powerful.
● They are so great because they remove the BIG question - “What to buy?”.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 42 of 53 --

43
Tip 5: Offer expedited or GUARANTEED per-Christmas delivery.
● Free shipping is the new normal, it will not move the needle.
● A real worry: people are genuinely worried about not getting their gifts in time.
● Combine the two. A combo of guaranteed per-Christmas delivery + free wrapping. Could you resist it? 😇
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 53 --

44
Two admin tips - regarding creative
1.) Submit your ads to review early.
- Schedule your campaigns in advance.
- At a minimum get your campaigns approved a day before the day of launch.
- Every year the same thing: a high number of ads that don't run at all or have very limited distribution. Yet, they aren't
rejected.
2.) Turn off site pop-ups.
- More often than not too hard to admin. Causing conflicting discount rates on ads VS the site.
- This just ads one more step to the buyer journey.
Q4 Creative Strategieswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 53 --

45
Keeping your garden free from weeds ✅
You have 3 gardens to take care of:
1.) Business Manager
2.) Website
3.) Business Operations
Q4 Gardening www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 53 --

46
1.) Business Manager
Remove bid caps, if they are too low.
Q4 Gardening www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 53 --

47
1.) Business Manager
● Triple check your Spend Limit - make sure you don't hit your daily or monthly caps.
● Credit Allocation (if applicable) - ensure that your credit line will sustain your spend during the holidays.
● Don't change your credit card on file during holidays.
● Double check that you have accepted all ToS inside BM.
● Have at least 2 admins just in case someone gets locked out.
Q4 Gardening www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 47 of 53 --

48
2.) Website
● Keep a regular check on pixel working properly - a misfiring pixel can completely blow out your campaign.
● Mystery shop on your own site. Check that everything is seamless and compare to leading sites.
Q4 Gardening www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 48 of 53 --

49
3.) Business Operations
Add extra man-power to be on top of your messaging inboxes
to have a fast response time.
Q4 Gardening www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 49 of 53 --

50
3.) Business Operations
● Automate your operations: e.g. have you set up automated PDF reports, custom rules, data sync and automatic post
promotion to save you some valuable time?
● Do all team members have the required access and permissions to ad accounts and pages.
● Set up a "Holiday out of office protocol" among your team members.
Q4 Gardening www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
